concepts.title: Aggregate Demand is the main determinant of Employment
concepts.description: According to the theory of employment, the level of employment is determined by the level of aggregate demand in the economy. Aggregate demand includes consumption, investment, government expenditure, and net export.

concepts.title: The Multiplier Effect
concepts.description: The multiplier effect refers to the phenomenon where an initial increase in aggregate demand leads to a greater increase in the final level of employment, due to the successive rounds of spending and income generation.

concepts.title: Keynesian Cross Diagram
concepts.description: The Keynesian Cross diagram is a graphical representation of the relationship between the level of employment and the level of aggregate demand. It shows how an increase in aggregate demand leads to an increase in employment.

concepts.title: AD-AS Framework
concepts.description: To remember the AD-AS framework, use the mnemonic 'AD' as 'Aggregate Demand' and 'AS' as 'Aggregate Supply'. The AD curve shifts to the right with an increase in aggregate demand, while the AS curve shifts to the right with an increase in aggregate supply.

concepts.title: Confusing Aggregate Demand with Aggregate Supply
concepts.description: Students often confuse aggregate demand with aggregate supply. Aggregate demand refers to the total amount of spending in the economy, while aggregate supply refers to the total amount of production in the economy.

concepts.title: Marginal Propensity to Consume (MPC) and Marginal Propensity to Save (MPS)
concepts.description: The Marginal Propensity to Consume (MPC) is the change in consumption resulting from a change in income, while the Marginal Propensity to Save (MPS) is the change in saving resulting from a change in income. The MPC + MPS = 1.

concepts.title: The Equilibrium Level of Employment
concepts.description: The equilibrium level of employment is the level of employment at which the aggregate demand curve intersects with the aggregate supply curve. At this level, the quantity of goods and services demanded equals the quantity of goods and services supplied.

concepts.title: The Multiplier
concepts.description: The multiplier is a measure of the increase in the final level of employment resulting from an initial increase in aggregate demand. It is calculated as 1 / (1 - MPC).
